spec_match.rationale: The demo uses 'font-size: fit-width' which is obsolete or incorrect; it should use the 'text-fit' property as defined in the spec and tested in conformance.

interactive.rationale: Good interactive controls including text input, color pickers, ON/OFF toggle, and a resizable preview frame.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The parent blurb aligns with the banner builder concept, though it inherits the 'font-size: fit-width' terminology error.

edge_cases.rationale: Demonstrates ON/OFF states, but the ON state is implemented with incorrect/non-standard CSS syntax.

references.rationale: The page is missing the mandatory ChromeStatus reference link in its see also section.

design_system.rationale: Adheres to the design system using appropriate CSS variables for custom colors and monospace styling.

openQuestions.title: Align with spec: Use text-fit property instead of font-size: fit-width
openQuestions.detail: The demo currently uses `font-size: fit-width` in both CSS and JS to scale the headline. However, the spec and other demos use the `text-fit` property (e.g., `text-fit: scale-down` or `text-fit: balance`), and the conformance suite tests `text-fit: width`. The demo should be updated to use the correct `text-fit` property to match the spec.

openQuestions.title: Add ChromeStatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: The page is missing the required reference link to `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5104141688635392`. It should be added to the 'see also' section to conform to the repository rules.

summary: While the interactive builder UI is well-designed, the demo fails on two critical repository rules: it uses an incorrect/obsolete syntax ('font-size: fit-width' instead of the 'text-fit' property) and it completely lacks the mandatory ChromeStatus reference link.
